title: Less Serious Offenses and Penalties under the Revised POEA Rules and Regulations
content:
This section enumerates less serious offenses for overseas Filipino workers under the Revised POEA Rules and Regulations. The offenses include:
- Unjustified breach of employment contract.
- Embezzlement of company funds or monies and/or properties of a fellow worker entrusted for delivery to kin or relatives in the Philippines.
- Violation of the religious and cultural practices of the host country.
- Violation of company policies and regulations.
- Insubordination or refusal to obey a lawful order of the employer or the duly authorized representative.
- Failure to refund the cost of repatriation advanced by the principal or recruitment agency, where termination of employment was due to the worker's own fault as determined by final judgment.
- Violation of the Code of Discipline for Overseas Filipino Workers.

Penalties for these offenses are progressive based on the number of offenses committed:
- 1st Offense: Suspension from participation in the overseas employment program for two months to six months.
- 2nd Offense: Suspension from participation in the overseas employment program for six months and one day to one year.
- 3rd Offense: Permanent disqualification from participation in the overseas employment program.
